proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_015 (continuous, 30-90 range, plausibly age-like) is associated with longer pfs_months in this NSCLC cohort.
Squamous histology (feature_006=='squamous') is associated with shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma.
Current smokers (feature_001=='current') have shorter pfs_months than former or never smokers.
Higher feature_014 (ordered 0/1/2, plausibly performance-status-like) is associated with monotonically shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.pearsonr(df.feature_015, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.757 between feature_015 and pfs_months; very strong positive correlation across 50,000 patients.
Mean PFS adenocarcinoma=3.670 mo vs squamous=2.813 mo, diff=+0.857 mo favoring adenocarcinoma.
Mean PFS: former=3.607 mo, current=2.987 mo, never=3.675 mo. Current smokers have ~0.62 mo shorter PFS than former; never vs former virtually identical (F=433, p~3.7e-187).
Mean PFS by feature_014 level: 0=4.327 mo, 1=3.185 mo, 2=2.152 mo; clean monotone decrease ~1.1 mo per level (consistent with ECOG performance status).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_023=0 in main effect.
feature_011=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_011=0.
feature_016=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_016=0.
feature_018=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_018=0 on average.
feature_028=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_028=0.
feature_017 (continuous) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_025 (continuous, mean ~3.8) is positively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: feature_023=1: 2.925 mo vs feature_023=0: 4.362 mo, diff -1.44 mo (p<1e-300 in OLS).
feature_011=1: 2.743 mo vs feature_011=0: 3.663 mo, diff -0.92 mo.
feature_016=1: 4.114 mo vs feature_016=0: 3.332 mo, diff +0.78 mo.
feature_018=1: 3.641 mo vs feature_018=0: 3.319 mo, diff +0.32 mo overall (modest, but heterogeneous as later confirmed).
feature_028=1: 2.661 mo vs 0: 3.472 mo, diff -0.81 mo (rare, 5%).
Pearson r=-0.127 between feature_017 and pfs_months; small but highly significant negative correlation.
Pearson r=+0.099; weak positive correlation between feature_025 and pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An OLS regression of pfs_months on all features simultaneously will retain feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_016, feature_018, feature_025, feature_017, smoking-current, and squamous histology as significant adjusted predictors of PFS.

feature_012, feature_020, and feature_027 will have negligible adjusted main effects on pfs_months despite being binary indicators (suggesting they may be randomly assigned exposures with no main effect).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS on all features yields R^2=0.894. Significant adjusted coefficients (units = months PFS): feature_015=+0.168 per unit, feature_014=-1.10 per level, feature_023=-1.42, feature_011=-0.91, feature_016=+0.89, feature_018=+0.32, feature_025=+0.46 per unit, feature_017=-0.073 per unit, smoking-current=-0.55, squamous histology=-0.72. All p<1e-30.

Adjusted coefficients: feature_012=-0.006 (p=0.39), feature_020=+0.002 (p=0.74), feature_027=+0.001 (p=0.88). All consistent with zero adjusted main effect, consistent with random assignment having no overall benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features, those whose prevalence is approximately constant (max-min spread <0.03) across all major strata (feature_006, feature_001, feature_014, and the other binaries) are randomly assigned, treatment-like indicators; specifically feature_012 (50%), feature_018 (35%), feature_020/027 (30%), feature_011 (25%), feature_002 (15%), feature_005 (3%), feature_023 (65%), feature_031 (45%) qualify.

Among the binary features, feature_022, feature_016, feature_021, and feature_028 will show large prevalence variation across strata (>0.07 spread), implying they are non-random patient characteristics (biomarkers / clinical features).

analyses.code: for each binary, compute max-min of its prevalence across strata of every other binary/categorical
analyses.result_summary: Spread across strata (smaller = more 'randomized-looking'): feature_005=0.006, feature_023=0.008, feature_002=0.010, feature_031=0.010, feature_018=0.012, feature_011=0.013, feature_020=0.026, feature_027=0.019, feature_012=0.020. All <0.03. Consistent with random assignment.

Spread feature_022=0.482 (51% in never-smokers vs 3% in current smokers, 7% squamous vs 15% adeno); feature_016=0.177; feature_021=0.370; feature_028=0.078. These are clearly NOT randomized; they correlate with histology/smoking and are likely biomarkers or clinical conditions.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity exists for feature_012 (the most balanced 50/50 binary): it interacts with one or more features to produce subgroup-level PFS differences.

analyses.code: for f in features: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ feature_012 * {f}')
analyses.result_summary: feature_012 has no large interaction terms (best is feature_017 at p=3.2e-4 with coef +0.018; next feature_023 at p=6.8e-3). Across 33 tested interactions with Bonferroni cutoff ~0.0015, only feature_017 is borderline. feature_012 is consistent with a randomized indicator with NO clinically meaningful effect, main or heterogeneous.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 is a treatment indicator whose effect on pfs_months is heterogeneous: scanning all feature_018 x feature_X interactions, at least one feature will show interaction p<<1e-10 with effect size >2 months.

analyses.code: for f in features: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ feature_018 * {f}')
analyses.result_summary: feature_018 x feature_016 interaction coefficient = +2.558 months (p ~ 0). Next strongest: feature_018 x feature_031 = -0.562 (p=5.3e-41). feature_018 x feature_028 = -0.488 (p=4.1e-7). feature_018 x feature_005 = significant in stratified analysis. Strong evidence of biomarker-defined heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stratified by feature_016, the feature_018 effect on pfs_months is null in feature_016=0 patients and large positive in feature_016=1 patients (textbook biomarker-targeted-therapy pattern).

analyses.result_summary: feature_016=0 (n=43,632, 87%): feature_018=1 mean PFS=3.329 vs =0 mean=3.334; diff=-0.005, p=0.80 — NULL effect. feature_016=1 (n=6,368, 13%): feature_018=1 mean=5.766 vs =0 mean=3.213; diff=+2.55 mo, p=2.5e-275. The +0.32 month overall effect of feature_018 is entirely driven by the 13% feature_016=1 subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_016=1 patients, feature_031=1 abolishes the feature_018 PFS benefit (resistance-modifier-like pattern).
Within feature_016=1 patients, feature_028=1 abolishes the feature_018 PFS benefit.
Within feature_016=1 patients, feature_005=1 abolishes the feature_018 PFS benefit.

analyses.code: Within d[d.feature_016==1], OLS pfs ~ feature_018 * X for each X
analyses.result_summary: feature_018 x feature_031 within feature_016=1: interaction coef=-4.66, p~0. Stratified: feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 (n=3508): tx=7.876 vs ctrl=3.232, diff=+4.64 mo, p~0. feature_016=1 & feature_031=1 (n=2860): tx=3.179 vs ctrl=3.191, diff=-0.012, p=0.88 — feature_031=1 completely cancels treatment benefit.

feature_018 x feature_028 within feature_016=1: coef=-3.02, p=2.9e-18. feature_016=1 & feature_028=0 (n=6109): tx-ctrl=+2.67 mo. feature_016=1 & feature_028=1 (n=259): tx-ctrl=-0.35 mo, p=0.23 — small subgroup but treatment effect abolished.

feature_018 x feature_005 within feature_016=1: coef=-2.55, p=2.3e-10. feature_016=1 & feature_005=0 (n=6177): tx-ctrl=+2.63 mo. feature_016=1 & feature_005=1 (n=191): tx-ctrl=+0.08 mo, p=0.81 — abolished.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A 3-way interaction model pfs_months ~ feature_018 * feature_016 * feature_031 will show: a near-zero baseline feature_018 main effect, a near-zero feature_018 x feature_031 interaction, but a large positive feature_018 x feature_016 interaction (~+4.6) and a near-equally large negative 3-way interaction (~-4.6) cancelling the benefit when feature_031=1.

analyses.result_summary: Coefficients: feature_018 main = -0.019 (p=0.50, null); feature_018:feature_016 = +4.66 (p~0); feature_018:feature_031 = +0.032 (p=0.46, null); feature_018:feature_016:feature_031 = -4.69 (p~0). Confirms biomarker-defined benefit (feature_016=1) that is fully abolished when feature_031=1. Adjusted (adding feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_025, feature_017, smoking, hist) yields nearly identical 2-way (+4.59) and 3-way (-4.58) coefficients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup definition for feature_018 PFS benefit is: feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0; within this subgroup feature_018 produces a >+4 month PFS gain, while outside the subgroup feature_018 produces ~0 effect.

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup n=3266 (6.5% of cohort): feature_018=1 mean PFS=8.234 vs feature_018=0 mean=3.249, diff=+4.985 months, p~0. Complement (everyone else, n=46,734): tx=3.317 vs ctrl=3.323, diff=-0.006, p=0.76. The feature_018 benefit is completely localised to this 4-feature subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 is a randomly assigned indicator (balanced ~65% across all strata) with a homogeneous, large negative main effect on pfs_months and no clinically large biomarker-defined heterogeneity.

analyses.code: Stratified means + interaction screen for feature_023
analyses.result_summary: Stratified feature_023=1 vs =0 effect on PFS: by feature_001 (former -1.43, current -1.43, never -1.48); by feature_006 (adeno -1.45, squamous -1.38); by feature_014 (0=-1.53, 1=-1.43, 2=-1.31); by feature_016 (0=-1.44, 1=-1.42). All effect sizes within 0.2 mo of each other; no large biomarker-defined modification. Largest interaction is feature_023 x feature_015 (coef -0.013/yr, p=2e-25) — modest amplification with higher feature_015. feature_023 acts as a strong, broadly homogeneous treatment-like indicator with PFS-shortening effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (balanced 25%) has a homogeneous negative main effect of ~-0.9 mo on PFS, with modest amplification at higher feature_015 (more harm in older patients).

analyses.result_summary: Best interaction: feature_011 x feature_015 coef=-0.0114, p=4.7e-15 (small modulation). Other interactions all p>0.001. Adjusted coef of feature_011 in full model = -0.91 mo. Conclusion: feature_011 acts as a randomly assigned PFS-reducing factor with minor modification by feature_015.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 is correlated with never-smoker status and is enriched in adenocarcinoma; despite its main positive effect on PFS, it does not modify feature_018 treatment effect (i.e. feature_022 is a covariate / natural feature, not a predictive biomarker).

analyses.result_summary: feature_022 prevalence: 51% in never-smokers vs 3% in current vs 8% in former; 15% in adeno vs 7% in squamous. Strongly correlated with smoking. Within feature_022=1 (n=6517): feature_018 effect tx=3.668 vs ctrl=3.556, diff=+0.11 mo (negligible). feature_022 is not a predictive biomarker for feature_018.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (smoking) and feature_006 (histology) effects on pfs_months are additive, with no significant smoking x histology interaction.

analyses.result_summary: All four interaction p-values >0.20 (smoking-former x squamous p=0.23; smoking-never x squamous p=0.49). Mean PFS: adeno+former=3.80, adeno+current=3.31, adeno+never=3.71; squamous+former=3.02, squamous+current=2.59, squamous+never=3.07. The smoking and histology effects are largely additive with no detectable interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 acts as an ordered, monotone PFS predictor: each unit increase reduces mean PFS by ~1.1 months (consistent with ECOG performance status).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.327 / 3.185 / 2.152 mo at levels 0/1/2 respectively. Linear-in-level OLS coefficient = -1.10 mo per unit (adjusted, p~0). Clean monotone gradient — confirms ECOG-PS-like ordering.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_017 (continuous, mean 3.84, max 28) is a tumor-burden-like predictor: higher values reduce PFS even after adjusting for stage/PS-like variables.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_017 coef = -0.073 mo per unit, p~0. Independent of feature_014 (PS-like) and feature_023 (treatment-like). Plausibly a lab marker (LDH, NLR, tumor burden).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A regression-tree-based estimator of the conditional average treatment effect (CATE) of feature_018 will identify feature_016 and feature_031 as the dominant splitters, and patients with predicted CATE>4 mo will be 100% feature_016=1 and 100% feature_031=0.

analyses.code: T-learner: DecisionTreeRegressor(max_depth=4) on feature_018=1 vs =0 cohorts; predict CATE = T1 - T0 for each patient
analyses.result_summary: CATE distribution: median=+0.62, q75=+0.66, max=+5.78. Patients with predicted CATE >4 mo (n=938, 1.9%): 100% feature_016=1, 100% feature_031=0. Patients with predicted CATE >2 mo (n=3663): 100% feature_016=1, 68% feature_031=0. Tree top splits in feature_018=1 cohort use feature_015 and feature_016. Confirms parametric-model-derived subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_020 (balanced 30%) has neither a significant main effect nor large heterogeneous treatment-like effect on pfs_months — it is a randomly assigned indicator with no clinical impact.
feature_027 (balanced 30%) has neither a significant main effect nor large heterogeneous effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main effect feature_020=+0.002 (p=0.74). Interaction screen: best feature_020 x feature_029 p=0.014 (modest); none survive multiple-test correction. feature_020 effectively null.
Adjusted main effect feature_027=+0.001 (p=0.88). Best interaction feature_027 x feature_028 p=0.017; not significant after correction. feature_027 effectively null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Most of the continuous lab-like features (feature_003, feature_004, feature_007, feature_008, feature_009, feature_010, feature_013, feature_019, feature_024, feature_026, feature_029, feature_030, feature_033) have no detectable main effect on pfs_months and no clinically large interaction with feature_018.

analyses.code: Univariate Pearson r and adjusted regression coefficients
analyses.result_summary: All listed features have |r|<0.01 with PFS and adjusted OLS coefficients indistinguishable from zero (all |t|<2). None show feature_018 interactions surviving multiple-test correction. They appear to be noise-like or near-balanced lab features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_031 is balanced as a randomly assigned indicator but its modest negative main effect (-0.20 mo adjusted) reflects a population-average, not biomarker-driven, harm; its primary mechanistic role is as a feature_018 effect MODIFIER specifically in feature_016=1 patients.

analyses.code: Compare feature_031 main effect in feature_016=0 vs feature_016=1 strata
analyses.result_summary: feature_031 main effect: in feature_016=0 patients, feature_031=1 vs =0 mean PFS = 3.235 vs 3.413 (diff -0.18). In feature_016=1 patients (controls only), feature_031=1 vs =0 mean PFS = 3.191 vs 3.232 (diff -0.04, NS). The feature_031 'harm' is small and homogeneous in feature_016=0; in feature_016=1 it's near-zero in controls but large among feature_018-treated patients (cancelling the +4.6 mo benefit). Confirms feature_031 is principally a feature_018-resistance modifier in feature_016+ patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding the 4-feature subgroup interaction (feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0) x feature_018 to the multivariable adjusted PFS model substantially improves fit beyond the simple feature_018 main effect.

analyses.code: Compare R^2 of two nested OLS models on adjusted covariate set (feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_025, feature_017, smoking, histology) with vs without the 4-way subgroup x feature_018 interaction
analyses.result_summary: Base adjusted model R^2 ~0.894. Adding the subgroup x feature_018 interaction adds clearly significant explanatory power: subgroup x feature_018 coefficient = +4.99 mo, t > 100, p~0. The simple feature_018 main effect (+0.32) collapses once the subgroup is included.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_002 (balanced 15%) has no clinically meaningful main effect or feature_018 interaction.
feature_005 (rare, 3%) has a modest main negative effect on PFS but its primary action is as a feature_018-resistance modifier within feature_016=1.

analyses.result_summary: feature_002 unadjusted means 3.430 vs 3.439 (diff +0.009, p=0.75). Adjusted coef -0.019, p=0.04 — borderline; clinically negligible. No important feature_018 interaction.

analyses.code: Adjusted regression and feature_018 interaction within feature_016=1
analyses.result_summary: feature_005 adjusted main coef -0.110 (p<1e-7). Within feature_016=1 cohort feature_005=1 abolishes feature_018 benefit (diff +0.08 mo vs +2.63 in feature_005=0). Confirms refined role.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After accounting for the feature_018 x feature_016 x (feature_031, feature_028, feature_005) heterogeneity, no other binary feature shows a strong second-line treatment-effect heterogeneity on pfs_months (i.e. feature_018 is the sole treatment with predictive heterogeneity in this dataset).

analyses.code: For each of feature_012/020/027/011/023/031/002/005, compute interaction coefficient with all features in d[d.feature_016==0] (where feature_018 has no effect, isolating other treatments)
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=0: feature_012 has best interaction p=5.7e-4 with feature_017 (coef +0.018) — very small magnitude. feature_023, feature_011, feature_031 effects remain homogeneous across feature_016=0 strata. No alternative binary shows a >2-month subgroup effect. feature_018 is the unique treatment with strong predictive heterogeneity in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028 has a negative main effect on PFS that holds in feature_016=0 patients (consistent with a generic harmful clinical condition) AND additionally abolishes the feature_018 benefit in feature_016=1 patients (dual role).

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=0: feature_028=1 mean PFS=2.63 vs =0 mean=3.39 (diff -0.76, p<1e-30). Within feature_016=1 controls: feature_028=1 mean=2.43 vs =0 mean=3.25 (diff -0.81). Within feature_016=1 treated: feature_028=1 mean=2.08 vs =0 mean=5.91 (diff -3.83). So feature_028 has a homogeneous baseline -0.8 mo harm AND additionally cancels the +4.8 feature_018 benefit when both feature_016=1 and feature_028=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL synthesis: pfs_months in this NSCLC cohort is best modelled as: a strong gradient on feature_015 (+0.17/unit) and feature_014 (-1.1/level); fixed harms from feature_023 (-1.4), feature_011 (-0.9), squamous histology (-0.7), current smoking (-0.55), feature_017 (-0.07/unit); and a single dramatic treatment effect of feature_018 of +5.0 months PFS, gated by the complete subgroup (feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0; n=3,266; ~6.5% of cohort), with effectively zero feature_018 effect outside this subgroup.

analyses.code: Final adjusted model summary + subgroup contrast
analyses.result_summary: Full adjusted OLS R^2=0.894 with feature_015 (+0.168), feature_014 (-1.10), feature_023 (-1.42), feature_011 (-0.91), feature_016 (+0.89), feature_018 (+0.32 average, but heterogeneous), feature_025 (+0.46), feature_017 (-0.073), smoking-current (-0.55), squamous (-0.72) all p<<1e-50. In the targeted subgroup (feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 & feature_028=0 & feature_005=0, n=3,266): feature_018=1 PFS=8.234 vs feature_018=0 PFS=3.249, diff=+4.985 mo (p~0). In the complement (n=46,734): diff=-0.006 mo (p=0.76). The treatment is essentially binary in benefit: ~+5 mo PFS only inside the subgroup; nothing outside.
